LanguageData.VPE_Bileceps.description: Harvested bileceps. Can be cooked into meals or eaten raw.
LanguageData.VPE_Bileceps.label: raw bileceps
LanguageData.VPE_Blisscap.description: Raw blisscap obtained by harvesting blisscap mushrooms. When eaten, makes people incredibly high, but also incredibly drowsy.
LanguageData.VPE_Blisscap.label: raw blisscap
LanguageData.VPE_Building_Zapcap.description: A large, bulbous, beige mushroom that is known to produce small amounts of electricity as it grows. Needs to be placed on power conduits in order to use the newly produced energy. In case of an electrical short, zapcaps amplify the released energy, causing significantly more damage.
LanguageData.VPE_Building_Zapcap.label: zapcap
LanguageData.VPE_Mycothread.description: Cotton-like, edible fabric extracted from mycothread mushrooms. It’s soft to the touch and easy to work with, deteriorates quickly, has an unusual color, and oddly still smells and tastes like mushrooms.
LanguageData.VPE_Mycothread.label: mycothread
LanguageData.VPE_Plant_Bileceps.description: Bileceps is a peculiar, coral-like mushroom that thrives on the bile of decaying bodies. Despite its unappetizing source, it possesses a meaty flavor and offers a protein-rich harvest. Its distinctive, cancerous appearance does little to deter colonists from utilizing it as an alternate source of meat.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Requires corpse bile on its tile to grow properly. Time to get a Helixien slug!
LanguageData.VPE_Plant_Bileceps.label: bileceps
LanguageData.VPE_Plant_Blisscap.description: A long, light-blue mushroom that offers more than just sustenance. Eating it induces a state of blissful happiness, making the consumer exceptionally content, yet notably drowsy. It is known to greatly enhance the psychic sensitivity of whoever eats it, and is often consumed by hippie communities on various core planets, allowing them to ‘see into the future’.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Contrary to other plants, blisscap ignores light levels for growth. Sunlight won't destroy it. It will still rest during the night period.
LanguageData.VPE_Plant_Blisscap.label: blisscap
LanguageData.VPE_Plant_Foampuffer.description: An ugly, thick mushroom with a cap that smells of muffalo fecal matter. It needs to be grown in flower pots. If it comes into contact with fire once matured, it explodes, ejecting a spray of firefoam over a short distance.
LanguageData.VPE_Plant_Foampuffer.label: foampuffer
LanguageData.VPE_Plant_Glowcap.description: A small blue mushroom typically found in caves. The smell it emits is absolutely repulsive - but it tastes surprisingly good when cooked. It lights up the area around it, but the light is not strong enough to grow crops.
LanguageData.VPE_Plant_Glowcap.label: glowcap
LanguageData.VPE_Plant_Healtruffle.description: Healtruffle is a mushroom that grows slowly by using nutrients from rotstink - it requires a supply of rotstink gas around it in order to grow. Harvesting it gives you herbal medicine. However, it takes a lot of work to plant and collect because it's very delicate. It's been grown for a long time by settlers for its medicinal properties.
LanguageData.VPE_Plant_Healtruffle.label: healtruffle
LanguageData.VPE_Plant_Lullabloom.description:
A special kind of mushroom that emits calming spores, ideal for planting next to a bed. Needs to be planted in a flower pot. Its presence soothes the sleeping individual, creating a more restful slumber and promoting overall comfort. Its gentle, sleep-inducing properties make it a favorite among colonists seeking a peaceful night's rest.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Contrary to other plants, lullabloom ignores light levels for growth. Sunlight won't destroy it. It will still rest during the night period.

Nullifies the "disturbed sleep" thought.

Will link to vanilla and Vanilla Furniture Expanded beds.
LanguageData.VPE_Plant_Lullabloom.label: lullabloom
LanguageData.VPE_Plant_MycothreadMushroom.description: This mushroom has been cultivated for ages by tunneler mining colonies, as it produces a thick but flexible cotton-like fabric which can be used to craft ropes, clothing and furniture. While it takes an incredibly long time to grow, and dies when exposed to any sort of light, for some colonies it is the only reliable way of obtaining good quality textiles.
LanguageData.VPE_Plant_MycothreadMushroom.label: mycothread mushroom
LanguageData.VPE_Plant_Redcap.description: A cultivated mushroom with a beautiful red cap and a somewhat short lifespan. Recaps are beautiful, but may need to be replanted often.
LanguageData.VPE_Plant_Redcap.label: redcap
LanguageData.VPE_Plant_Stinker.description: A small, fast-growing mushroom that forms large patches. It produces rotstink through a chemical process. Despite its unpleasant odor, it can be harvested for edible fungus. It can grow anywhere, however exposure to sunlight will kill it.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Contrary to other mushrooms, stinkers can handle light source up to 50% luminosity, such as torches.
LanguageData.VPE_Plant_Stinker.label: stinker
LanguageData.VPE_Plant_Thermoregulus.description: A rare, large mushroom that needs to be grown in flower pots. Once mature, thermoregulus emits varying temperature depending on the ambient temperature around it. At all times it will aim to bring the temperature of the room it’s in to 16C. \n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Contrary to other mushroom, sunlight won't destroy it.
LanguageData.VPE_Plant_Thermoregulus.label: thermoregulus
LanguageData.VPE_Plant_Timbercap.description: A short, brown mushroom with a hard stem. When harvested, it yields a small amount of wood-like substance.
LanguageData.VPE_Plant_Timbercap.label: timbercap
LanguageData.VPE_Plant_Toxpuff.description: A small, green oval mushroom typically found in highly polluted areas. It puffs out tox gas when anyone steps on it.
LanguageData.VPE_Plant_Toxpuff.label: toxpuff
LanguageData.VPE_Plant_Zapcap.description: A large, bulbous, beige mushroom that is known to produce small amounts of electricity as it grows. Needs to be placed on power conduits in order to use the newly produced energy. In case of an electrical short, zapcaps amplify the released energy, causing significantly more damage.
LanguageData.VPE_Plant_Zapcap.label: zapcap
